expectations.text: Identifies CodeT5+ as a generative / seq2seq / encoder-decoder architecture (or otherwise invokes the body's scope rule about generative models). Must invoke a generalizable architectural property -- not just say "we don't support codet5p specifically." Note: codet5p is NOT named in the description's exclusion list, so this tests whether the body's scope rule generalizes.
expectations.evidence: Response identifies CodeT5+ as 'T5-style encoder-decoder seq2seq generator' and invokes the body's scope rule ('seq2seq generative models are explicitly out of scope').

expectations.text: States clearly that this model is out of scope for the winml pipeline today, not hedged with "might work" or "try and see."
expectations.evidence: Title: 'Stop — Salesforce/codet5p-220m is out of scope for the WinML ModelKit pipeline today'. Unhedged.

expectations.text: Does NOT walk the user through `winml export/optimize/quantize/compile/build/perf` for this model.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly refuses to walk the pipeline; lists what would fail at each stage if attempted.

expectations.text: Suggests a legitimate alternative for running this kind of model on Windows/NPU (e.g., onnxruntime-genai, Olive, ORT directly) OR mentions roadmap for generative support.
expectations.evidence: Alternatives proposed (ONNX Runtime GenAI, OpenVINO GenAI, AITK / Foundry Local) + late-2026 roadmap mention.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
